evals.prompt: This dashboard looks too plain and feels AI-made. Renew src/app/dashboard. The target users are SREs working night shifts.
evals.expected_output: In Phase 0, detect whether impeccable is installed and confirm the Design Context (audience=SRE, night operations context -> derive a dark theme). Then run Phase 2 slop audit -> Phase 3 renew (self-contained ruleset or impeccable polish) -> Phase 4 re-verification. Do not use Banned Output Patterns such as border-left stripes, gradient text, or Inter. Preserve behavior, routes, and information architecture. Output a renewal report.

Run check mode only without editing. Detect Part B/C signals from anti-slop-ruleset with grep and apply the AI Slop Test plus Part E required-quality counts. Attach ID, file:line, observed, why, and fix to every finding. Classify priority and explicitly output the --fail-on critical gate decision. Make no code changes.

evals.prompt: Audit this landing page design. Our company does not have impeccable installed.
evals.expected_output: Even when impeccable is not installed, do not refuse. Fall back to the self-contained anti-slop-ruleset and run the check. Do not rationalize that the task cannot be done because impeccable is missing. Output a slop audit report based on the local ruleset.

evals.prompt: The hero font is Inter and looks too generic. Renew it with a different direction.
evals.expected_output: Recognize Inter as banned, but do not simply swap to a reflex font such as Fraunces. Derive three brand words, evaluate candidate fonts as physical objects in the catalog, and apply a display/body font pairing. Reject the rationalization of replacing it with a second-favorite default.

evals.prompt: Check whether this screen has UX or accessibility (WCAG) issues.
evals.expected_output: Recognize that this request is about usability, information architecture, and WCAG, which is outside ywc-design-renew scope. Route or delegate to ywc-ui-ux-review. Preserve the boundary that this skill owns aesthetics and slop signals only.
